fr:mainmatter.fr:p: An encoding is a mapping from one language to another.

The source language is the encoded language and the target language is the encoding language.
An encoding is
faithful if it preserves the semantics of the encoded language
full if it preserves the meaning of the encoded language and the encoding language
compositional if it preserves the structure of the encoded language

Where formal languages are concerned, the relationship between the concrete syntax and the abstract syntax is an encoding. The parser is here the encoding function, while the pretty-printer is the decoding function.
